hypothesis: Reproduce frozen default on current code using official H5 split
compared_baseline: BRACS UNI MIR-MIL baseline val/test from existing artifacts
command: PYTHONPATH=$PWD mamba run -n mirmil python train_mil.py --yaml_path configs/experiments/MIR_MIL_BRACS_FROZEN_DEFAULT_UNI.yaml
interpretation: Incorrect reproduction protocol: baseline manifest used BRACS_uni_split_official_train_val.csv with PT paths, not the H5 mirror. H5 I/O was also too slow.
next_action: Use original baseline PT split for reproduction.

hypothesis: Current code should reproduce frozen-architecture default MIR-MIL on the original BRACS UNI train/val split
compared_baseline: Existing BRACS UNI MIR-MIL baseline; historical default/HPO seed2024 val around 0.817 in prior notes; official test baseline mean 0.7694
command: PYTHONPATH=$PWD mamba run -n mirmil python train_mil.py --yaml_path configs/experiments/MIR_MIL_BRACS_FROZEN_DEFAULT_UNI.yaml
interpretation: No validation improvement; lower than strongest historical default/capacity validation. This is not a candidate and suggests either code/config drift or high stochastic sensitivity.
keep_or_discard: discard for optimization; keep as current-code reproducibility evidence
next_action: Before new tuning, audit exact config drift against original baseline MIR_MIL yaml/checkpoint and run split/metric audit.

hypothesis: Verify official BRACS split, labels, baseline result table, and metric fields before further optimization
compared_baseline: Existing BRACS baseline matrix and official R50/UNI full split CSVs
command: PYTHONPATH=$PWD mamba run -n mirmil python experiments/audit_bracs_protocol.py --r50-split /data15/data15_5/fanhao/datasets/BRACS/MIRMIL_FEATURES/metadata/BRACS_r50_split_official_full.csv --uni-split /data15/data15_5/fanhao/datasets/BRACS/MIRMIL_FEATURES/metadata/BRACS_uni_split_official_full.csv --baseline-seed-results artifacts/bracs_evaluation/seed_results.csv --output reports/bracs_protocol_audit.json
interpretation: R50/UNI train/val/test counts match; path stems and labels match; no duplicate stems; baseline seed_results has required metric columns, finite metrics in [0,1], and exactly 3 seeds per feature/model.
next_action: Audit exact config drift against original baseline; then run the next validation-driven architecture-frozen experiment only if drift is understood.

hypothesis: Explain why historical default_refit validation is not directly comparable to official train/val recovery run
compared_baseline: Historical default_refit seed2024 vs official train/val frozen default
command: read-only comparison of saved YAML files and split CSV counts
interpretation: Historical default_refit used 460 training slides, 10 epochs, patience 100, cosine T_max 8; current official recovery used 395 training slides, 30 epochs, patience 8, T_max 28. The high refit validation AUC should not be treated as an official baseline or direct reproduction target.
next_action: Proceed with official 395/65 validation-driven architecture-frozen experiments; top hypothesis is sampling instability from large BRACS bags.

hypothesis: Test whether deterministic uniform training subsampling reduces BRACS large-bag sampling instability compared with random training subsampling
command: PYTHONPATH=$PWD mamba run -n mirmil python train_mil.py --yaml_path configs/experiments/MIR_MIL_BRACS_FROZEN_UNI_SAMPLING_UNIFORM.yaml
interpretation: Small validation improvement over current-code random reproduction, but still below historical official default-region validation and based on one seed only. This supports sampling sensitivity but is not enough to keep as a robust improvement.
keep_or_discard: candidate; do not claim; do not test
next_action: Run seed2025/2026 only if this remains the best validation-driven candidate after checking an equivalent R50/random-vs-uniform or a higher-priority early-stop/schedule alignment experiment.

hypothesis: Check seed robustness of the uniform-sampling candidate
command: PYTHONPATH=$PWD mamba run -n mirmil python train_mil.py --yaml_path configs/experiments/MIR_MIL_BRACS_FROZEN_UNI_SAMPLING_UNIFORM_SEED2025.yaml
interpretation: Seed2025 fails to reproduce the seed2024 validation gain and is below the current-code random seed2024 reproduction. Uniform sampling appears unstable and cannot be accepted after two seeds.
keep_or_discard: discard as robust improvement; keep as evidence for BRACS seed sensitivity
next_action: Do not open official test. Next compare R50/UNI default under identical current protocol or test a lower-risk schedule/early-stop alignment before more sampling variants.

hypothesis: Compare R50 and UNI under identical current frozen official train/val protocol
compared_baseline: BRACS_REPRO_DEFAULT_UNI_SEED2024_PT val_macro_auc=0.798629; historical R50 MIR-MIL official test mean 0.7087
command: PYTHONPATH=$PWD mamba run -n mirmil python train_mil.py --yaml_path configs/experiments/MIR_MIL_BRACS_FROZEN_DEFAULT_R50.yaml
interpretation: Under the current frozen protocol, R50 learns much more slowly and remains far below UNI validation. This rejects the idea that R50 is immediately more stable/better under the transferred UNI/default settings; R50 likely needs feature-specific optimizer/schedule tuning if pursued.
keep_or_discard: discard as improvement; keep as feature-behavior evidence
next_action: Do not test. Next high-value experiment should be a schedule/early-stop alignment or LR/WD narrow tune, separately for UNI first because UNI remains stronger under current protocol.

hypothesis: Diagnose whether BRACS underperformance is driven by 7-class fine-grained label difficulty by collapsing to official 3-class BT/AT/MT task
command: PYTHONPATH=$PWD mamba run -n mirmil python experiments/build_bracs_3class_split_csv.py && PYTHONPATH=$PWD mamba run -n mirmil python train_mil.py --yaml_path configs/experiments/MIR_MIL_BRACS3_FROZEN_DEFAULT_UNI.yaml
interpretation: Collapsing 7 classes to BRACS BT/AT/MT substantially improves validation under the same frozen UNI architecture and features. This supports the hypothesis that fine-grained BRACS labels and AT-category ambiguity are major failure drivers. Confusion still shows AT is the hardest class.
keep_or_discard: keep as diagnostic evidence, not 7-class SOTA evidence
next_action: Run seed2025/2026 for BRACS3 only if we want to quantify robustness of the diagnosis; do not compare this directly to 7-class SOTA.

hypothesis: Verify the generic class-aware evidence residual branch trains under the official BRACS3 train/val split without changing features or split

next_action: Run controlled multi-seed BRACS3/PANDA ablations only after code review and commit; use validation only for model selection.

next_action: Run a PANDA UNI matched sanity ablation. If PANDA does not materially drop, then evaluate BRACS3 official test once under the frozen command and report with reviewer-style caveats.

hypothesis: Evaluate the single validation-selected evidence_w005 candidate once on official BRACS3 test after PANDA sanity passed
compared_baseline: UNI MIR_MIL original official test macro_auc=0.827973; UNI MIR_MIL noES/bestval official test macro_auc=0.8403; UNI AC_MIL official test macro_auc=0.852852
command: mamba run -n mirmil python experiments/evaluate_checkpoints.py --run-root artifacts/bracs3_arch_ablation/uni/evidence_w005 --output-dir artifacts/bracs3_arch_ablation/uni/evidence_w005_official_test --models MIR_MIL --device 0 --num-workers 2 --group test --checkpoint-kind best --split-override /data15/data15_5/fanhao/datasets/BRACS/MIRMIL_FEATURES/metadata/BRACS3_uni_split_official_full.csv --wandb-mode disabled
val_metric: budget128 test_macro_auc=0.796290±0.016543; budget256 test_macro_auc=0.789243±0.015511; budget512 test_macro_auc=0.809526±0.014712
result: completed official test once; negative result
interpretation: Despite strong BRACS validation and no PANDA seed2024 validation drop, evidence_w005 fails to transfer to BRACS official test and is worse than original UNI MIR-MIL. This is direct evidence of BRACS validation over-selection and/or a harmful test-domain shift induced by class-aware evidence selection.
keep_or_discard: discard as accepted improvement; keep code as disabled ablation module
next_action: Do not claim SOTA. Do not tune evidence_weight on test. Next work should analyze why validation/test diverged, especially calibration/confusion and bag-size or domain shift, before trying another generic architecture.
